source: NHS England PRN02356 - Quality and Outcomes Framework guidance for 2026/27 (effective 1 April 2026). Underpinned by NICE indicator menu IDs where shown in indicator notes.
notes: Register membership uses substring matching on problem labels (approximation of QOF SNOMED refsets). Indicator checks use the most recent matching observation within the indicator window. Frailty exclusion is implemented via excludeIfProblem on each affected indicator. Some 26/27 indicators (DM037 multi-observation bundle, HF009 multi-drug AND logic) require rules-engine extensions and are shipped disabled with placeholder definitions. Retired 25/26 indicators are kept disabled to make the year-on-year diff visible. This is a memory aid only; not for QOF claim purposes. 2026-06-04 (The Keeper): added the NEW 26/27 Obesity clinical area — OB register (enabled, BMI-driven so substring-approximated) plus indicators OB004/OB005 shipped DISABLED as drafts pending CSO confirmation of points/thresholds against PRN02356.

rules.source: QOF 26/27 AST register. Underlying business rules amended for 26/27 to include patients from age 5 (was 6); Sentinel's substring match is age-agnostic so this change has no functional effect here.
QOF 26/27 COPD register. Underlying business rules amended for 26/27 to address potential under/over-recording; the SNOMED refset has been refined but Sentinel's substring match is unchanged.
QOF 26/27 MH register (SMI_COD refset approximation — schizophrenia, bipolar disorder, other psychoses)
QOF 26/27 OB register (NEW clinical area for 2026/27, NHS England PRN02356). DRAFT — corroborated via search, pending PRN02356 primary-source confirmation.
rules.notes: NEW for 26/27. The true QOF Obesity register is BMI-driven (adults with a BMI ≥30, or ≥27.5 for people of South Asian, Chinese, other Asian, Middle Eastern, Black African or African-Caribbean family background, recorded in the preceding 12 months) — it is NOT a problem-coded register. Sentinel approximates it here with substring problem-matching, which will MISS obese patients who have a recorded BMI but no 'obesity' problem label, and is age-agnostic. Proper membership needs a BMI-observation-based register (engine extension) before this should be relied on. Underpins indicators OB004/OB005 (shipped disabled pending confirmation).

rules.indicatorName: [RETIRED 25/26] BP <=140/90 in HYP <80
rules.notes: Retired in 26/27. Replaced by HYP010, which excludes patients with moderate or severe frailty.

rules.indicatorName: [RETIRED 25/26] BP <=150/90 in HYP >=80
rules.notes: Retired in 26/27. Replaced by HYP011, which excludes patients with moderate or severe frailty.

rules.indicatorName: [RETIRED 25/26] BP <=140/90 in CHD <80
rules.notes: Retired in 26/27. CHD and STIA BP indicators combined into CD001 (under 80, no frailty).

rules.indicatorName: [RETIRED 25/26] BP <=150/90 in CHD >=80
rules.notes: Retired in 26/27. Replaced by CD002 (combined CHD/STIA, ≥80, no frailty).

rules.indicatorName: [RETIRED 25/26] BP <=140/90 in STIA <80
rules.notes: Retired in 26/27. Replaced by CD001 (combined CHD/STIA).

rules.indicatorName: [RETIRED 25/26] BP <=150/90 in STIA >=80
rules.notes: Retired in 26/27. Replaced by CD002 (combined CHD/STIA).

rules.indicatorName: [RETIRED 25/26] HbA1c <=64 mmol/mol in DM
rules.notes: Retired in 26/27. Replaced by frailty-stratified DM020 (HbA1c ≤58, non-frail) and DM021 (HbA1c ≤75, frail).

rules.indicatorName: [RETIRED 25/26] HbA1c <=75 mmol/mol in DM
rules.notes: Retired in 26/27. The HbA1c ≤75 cohort is now DM021 (restricted to patients with moderate/severe frailty only).

rules.indicatorName: BP <=140/90 in CHD/Stroke/TIA <80 (no frailty)
rules.notes: New combined CHD/STIA BP indicator (41 points). Sentinel splits this into two register-specific rules (qof-cd001-chd and qof-cd001-stia) so the chip fires for patients on either register. Patients on both registers will see two chips, which is a known cosmetic limitation. HBPM equivalent target is 135/85.

rules.indicatorName: BP <=140/90 in CHD/Stroke/TIA <80 (no frailty)
rules.notes: STIA-register half of the combined CD001 indicator. Points value (41) is the total indicator allocation; do not double-count if patient sees both chips.

rules.indicatorName: BP <=150/90 in CHD/Stroke/TIA >=80 (no frailty)
rules.notes: New combined CHD/STIA BP indicator for ≥80 (20 points). HBPM equivalent target is 145/85.

rules.indicatorName: BP <=150/90 in CHD/Stroke/TIA >=80 (no frailty)
rules.notes: STIA-register half of the combined CD002 indicator.

rules.indicatorName: BP <=140/90 in HYP <80 (no frailty)
rules.notes: Replaces HYP008. Excludes moderate/severe frailty. HBPM equivalent 135/85.

rules.indicatorName: BP <=150/90 in HYP >=80 (no frailty)
rules.notes: Replaces HYP009. Excludes moderate/severe frailty. HBPM equivalent 145/85.

rules.indicatorName: Antiplatelet or anticoagulant in CHD
rules.notes: Secondary prevention. Aspirin, alternative antiplatelet (clopidogrel/ticagrelor/prasugrel) or anticoagulant all count.

rules.indicatorName: Antiplatelet or anticoagulant in Stroke/TIA
rules.notes: Ticagrelor newly added to the antiplatelet list for 26/27. For non-haemorrhagic stroke or history of TIA.

rules.indicatorName: Statin (or alternative LLT) in CVD/CKD register patients
rules.notes: Points reduced from 38 to 20 for 26/27 (consistency with other lipid indicators). Full QOF rule covers CHD, PAD, STIA, CKD registers; Sentinel approximates with CHD only until rules engine supports multi-register OR logic.

rules.indicatorName: Cholesterol target: LDL <=2.0 or non-HDL <=2.6 in CVD register
rules.notes: Unchanged in 26/27 (44 points, threshold 20-50). Full rule: LDL ≤2.0 OR non-HDL ≤2.6 mmol/L; LDL takes priority when both present on same date. Highest-value indicator. NICE NG238 aligned.

rules.indicatorName: CHA2DS2-VASc score recorded in preceding 12 months
rules.notes: CORRECTED for 26/27: the previous Sentinel rule labelled AF006 was actually checking for DOAC/warfarin presence, which is QOF AF008. This rule now correctly checks for a recorded CHA2DS2-VASc score. Upper threshold raised from 90 to 95 for 26/27. Excludes patients with any previous score ≥2.

rules.indicatorName: DOAC (or VKA if DOAC declined/unsuitable) in AF with CHA2DS2-VASc >=2
rules.notes: Renamed from previous Sentinel id qof-af006. NG196 prefers DOAC as first-line; warfarin counts where DOAC declined or clinically unsuitable. Sentinel cannot calculate CHA2DS2-VASc itself, so this rule fires for all AF patients on anticoagulant; clinician must confirm CHA2DS2-VASc ≥2 applies.

rules.indicatorName: ACE-I or ARB in diabetes with nephropathy/microalbuminuria
rules.notes: REPURPOSED for 26/27. The DM006 ID was reused: in 25/26 it meant 'HbA1c ≤58'; in 26/27 it now means 'ACE-I/ARB in diabetic nephropathy'. The old HbA1c ≤58 indicator is now DM020. Sentinel approximates by checking ACE-I/ARB presence in all DM patients; ideally should restrict to those with nephropathy/microalbuminuria codes on problem list — not currently expressible in the schema.

rules.indicatorName: Structured education within 9 months of new DM diagnosis
rules.notes: Applies to newly diagnosed DM in the QOF year. Sentinel cannot determine 'newly diagnosed' without diagnosis date, so this rule fires for all DM patients with a recent structured education code. Programme matches include DESMOND/DAFNE/X-PERT brand names commonly coded by primary care.

rules.indicatorName: HbA1c <=58 mmol/mol in DM (non-frail)
rules.notes: Replaces DM006 (25/26 meaning). Excludes patients with moderate or severe frailty. Threshold range widened to 35-75 (was 35-50).

rules.indicatorName: HbA1c <=75 mmol/mol in DM with moderate/severe frailty
rules.notes: Frailty-stratified replacement for DM008. Fires ONLY for patients on the DM register WITH moderate OR severe frailty coded — expressed via requiresAnyProblem (disjunctive: any one of the listed frailty levels). The engine now honours requiresAnyProblem (negation-aware), so this no longer over-triggers for all DM patients.

rules.indicatorName: Statin primary prevention in DM >=40 (no CVD, no frailty)
rules.notes: Primary prevention statin in DM patients ≥40 without CVD and without moderate/severe frailty. QOF rule additionally excludes T2DM with CVD risk score <10% in preceding 3 years; this nuance is not expressed here and may cause over-flagging in low-risk T2DM patients.

rules.indicatorName: Statin secondary prevention in DM with CVD history
rules.notes: Secondary prevention. Uses requiresAnyProblem — fires only if a DM register patient has at least one CVD problem coded (negation-aware, so 'family history of stroke' does not qualify). Excludes haemorrhagic stroke. The rules engine now honours requiresAnyProblem.

rules.indicatorName: BP <=140/90 in DM <=70 (no frailty)
rules.notes: Age threshold tightened for 26/27: was <80, now ≤70. Frailty exclusion retained.

rules.indicatorName: Eight diabetes care processes in preceding 12 months
rules.notes: NEW 26/27 indicator. Requires all 8 care processes in preceding 12 months: BMI, BP, HbA1c, cholesterol, smoking status, foot exam, ACR, eGFR/creatinine. Chip shows 'X/8 care processes' and is not_met until all 8 are recorded this QOF year. Smoking status is also surfaced separately via the qof-smok-dm chip. QOF year floor applies.

rules.indicatorName: Smoking status recorded in SMI (preceding 12 months)
rules.notes: Smoking rates in SMI are 2–3× the general population; cessation is the single highest-impact modifiable cardiovascular risk factor in this group. The QOF window uses the 1 Apr – 31 Mar QOF year floor. An encounter-coded journal entry is the typical evidence source; relies on Sentinel's journal endpoint fetch (v0.3.6+).

rules.indicatorName: Smoking status recorded in Asthma (preceding 12 months)
rules.source: QOF 26/27 SMOK001 (NICE IND112 / multiple disease registers)
rules.notes: Smoking is a major trigger and modifier of asthma; recording status annually enables cessation support. QOF year floor (1 Apr – 31 Mar) applies.

rules.indicatorName: Smoking status recorded in COPD (preceding 12 months)
rules.notes: Smoking cessation is the most effective COPD disease-modifying intervention available. QOF year floor applies. NRT and pharmacotherapy (varenicline, bupropion) should be offered.

rules.indicatorName: Smoking status recorded in Diabetes (preceding 12 months)
rules.notes: Also one of the eight DM037 care processes (currently disabled pending observation-bundle engine support). Annual recording drives cessation support and cardiovascular risk stratification. QOF year floor applies.

rules.indicatorName: Smoking status recorded in CHD (preceding 12 months)
rules.notes: Secondary prevention: cessation after MI/ACS significantly reduces reinfarction risk. QOF year floor applies.

rules.indicatorName: Smoking status recorded in Stroke/TIA (preceding 12 months)
rules.notes: Smoking is an independent risk factor for recurrent stroke. QOF year floor applies.

rules.indicatorName: Smoking status recorded in CKD (preceding 12 months)
rules.notes: Smoking accelerates CKD progression and amplifies cardiovascular risk. QOF year floor applies.

rules.indicatorName: Smoking status recorded in Heart Failure (preceding 12 months)
rules.notes: QOF year floor applies.

rules.indicatorName: Smoking status recorded in PAD (preceding 12 months)
rules.notes: Smoking cessation is the most important intervention in PAD — it halves the rate of progression to critical limb ischaemia. QOF year floor applies.

rules.indicatorName: HF review in preceding 12 months
rules.notes: Includes assessment of functional capacity and medication review. Encounter-coded entry; relies on Sentinel's journal endpoint fetch (added in v0.3.6).

rules.indicatorName: HF diagnosis confirmed by echo or specialist assessment
rules.notes: For HF diagnosed on or after 1 April 2023, confirmation by echo or specialist within 6 months of diagnosis (or registration). Sentinel cannot verify the timing window against diagnosis date, so this rule fires on presence of any echo/specialist code in record. Clinician must verify the timing rule.

rules.indicatorName: Four-pillar therapy in HFrEF (ACE-I/ARB/ARNI + BB + MRA + SGLT2i)
requiresAnyProblem: heart failure with reduced ejection fraction
rules.source: QOF 26/27 HF009 (NICE IND317, NG106 Sep 2025 update)
rules.notes: NEW 26/27 indicator. Replaces HF003 and HF006. Requires concurrent prescription from all 4 drug classes in HFrEF. DISABLED pending rules engine support for the medication-all-of (AND-across-groups) check kind. HFrEF subtype identification now uses requiresAnyProblem (any of the listed HFrEF synonyms), which the engine honours; the medication-all-of check is still the remaining blocker. Worth prioritising — 12 pts and clinically major change.

rules.indicatorName: Asthma review with 4 components in preceding 12 months
rules.notes: Threshold tightened for 26/27 from 50-75 to 45-70. The full QOF rule now requires the review to include FOUR components: (1) assessment of asthma control, (2) recording of number of exacerbations, (3) assessment of inhaler technique, (4) written personalised action plan. Sentinel cannot verify all four components are present; presence of an asthma-review code is the closest approximation. Clinician must verify the written action plan and other elements.

rules.indicatorName: Objective test within 3 months of new asthma diagnosis (post-Apr 2025)
rules.notes: NEW 26/27 indicator. Applies to patients newly diagnosed with asthma on or after 1 April 2025, with an objective test (spirometry with BDR, FeNO, or peak flow variability) within 3 months either side of diagnosis. Sentinel cannot determine diagnosis date or 3-month window without rules-engine extension — this rule fires for all asthma-register patients with any objective test in the last year. Useful as a prompt, not a claim verifier.

rules.indicatorName: COPD review in preceding 12 months with MRC dyspnoea
rules.notes: Includes record of number of exacerbations and MRC dyspnoea scale. Encounter-coded; relies on Sentinel's journal endpoint fetch.

rules.indicatorName: Falling eGFR trend
rules.notes: Clinical-review trend monitor, not a QOF claim indicator. Observation history is required: where only the latest eGFR is available the chip shows no_data until multi-point history exists. NICE NG203 defines accelerated CKD progression as a sustained decrease in eGFR of >=15 mL/min/1.73m2 within 12 months (or >=25% with a change in GFR category). Window is 24 months to capture enough GP data points (annual bloods mean 12 months yields only 1-2 readings). Confirm a sustained fall on repeat testing and exclude AKI before acting.

rules.indicatorName: Raised potassium (hyperkalaemia)
rules.source: NICE CKS Hyperkalaemia / UK Kidney Association (2020)
rules.notes: Hyperkalaemia clinical-safety alert. AMBER 5.5-5.9 mmol/L (mild): first exclude pseudohyperkalaemia (haemolysed or delayed sample) and recheck; review contributing drugs (ACEi/ARB, MRA/spironolactone, potassium-sparing diuretics, NSAIDs, trimethoprim, potassium supplements) and dietary potassium. RED >=6.0 mmol/L (moderate to severe): arrange urgent same-day clinical assessment and a 12-lead ECG. K+ >=6.5 mmol/L, or any ECG changes (peaked T waves, broadened QRS), is a medical emergency - give IV calcium gluconate for cardioprotection, insulin-glucose +/- salbutamol, continuous ECG monitoring and urgent senior/renal input. Always identify and treat the underlying cause.

rules.indicatorName: Rising HbA1c trend (diabetes)
rules.source: NICE NG28 (type 2 diabetes in adults) / NICE NG17 (type 1 diabetes)
rules.notes: Clinical-review trend monitor for worsening glycaemic control in people on the diabetes register (fires only when the patient is on the DM register). Not a QOF claim indicator. Values are in mmol/mol (IFCC units). Multi-point HbA1c history is required: where only the latest result is available the chip shows no_data until history exists. Window is 24 months to capture enough readings (HbA1c is typically checked 3-6 monthly in diabetes). The >=10 mmol/mol rise is a pragmatic review threshold, not a fixed guideline cut-off - adjust to local policy. A sustained rise suggests deteriorating control: review adherence, diet/lifestyle, intercurrent illness or steroids, and consider treatment intensification per NICE NG28/NG17. Confirm on repeat testing and interpret alongside the individualised HbA1c target (commonly 48 mmol/mol, or 53 mmol/mol where treatment carries hypoglycaemia risk).

rules.indicatorName: [DRAFT 26/27 — PENDING CONFIRMATION] Offer of referral to a weight-management programme in obesity
rules.source: QOF 26/27 OB004 (NHS England PRN02356) — DRAFT, corroborated via search, NOT yet confirmed against the primary guidance.
rules.notes: NEW 26/27 indicator, shipped DISABLED pending CSO confirmation of the exact points (corroborated ~5), thresholds (corroborated 10–30%) and code cluster against PRN02356. The true definition requires the referral to be offered within 90 days of the qualifying BMI being recorded — Sentinel cannot verify that 90-day timing, so this observation-recent approximation checks only for a weight-management-referral code within the QOF year. Flip enabled:true once the values are confirmed. Depends on the OB register, which is itself a BMI-driven approximation.

rules.indicatorName: [DRAFT 26/27 — PENDING CONFIRMATION] Weight-management pharmacotherapy / shared decision-making in obesity
rules.source: QOF 26/27 OB005 (NHS England PRN02356); tirzepatide eligibility per NICE TA1026 — DRAFT, corroborated via search, NOT yet confirmed against the primary guidance.
rules.notes: NEW 26/27 indicator, shipped DISABLED pending CSO confirmation of the exact points (corroborated ~13), thresholds (corroborated 50–80%) and definition against PRN02356. The full QOF definition also requires a shared-decision-making conversation and behavioural-support referral, which Sentinel cannot verify — this rule approximates by checking for weight-management pharmacotherapy only, so it will under-count patients managed by shared decision-making without a drug. Tirzepatide (Mounjaro) eligibility is BMI/comorbidity-gated per NICE TA1026 and cannot be verified here. Relevant to this dispensing practice's GLP-1 weight-loss prescribing. Flip enabled:true once values are confirmed.
